highlight.title: Cloud Run GPU Support and Multi-Container Jobs Now Generally Available
highlight.summary: GCP has made GPU configuration for Cloud Run services and multi-container sidecar support for Cloud Run jobs generally available, opening new possibilities for AI/ML workloads and complex service architectures. The update also includes Identity-Aware Proxy direct integration without load balancers.

resources.title: Compute Flexible Committed Use Discounts Now Cover Cloud Run
resources.why_now: A major cost optimization update allowing Cloud Run services with CPU always allocated to benefit from flexible CUDs, potentially reducing compute costs by up to 57% across Compute Engine, GKE, and Cloud Run with a single commitment.

resources.title: GCP Cost Optimization: 7 Essential Tactics for 2026
resources.why_now: With cloud costs growing 20-21% year over year and 20-40% of resources remaining underutilized, this comprehensive guide covers Spot VMs, right-sizing, and automated cost optimization strategies specifically relevant for dynamic Cloud Run and GKE workloads.

resources.title: Secret Manager Rotation with Pub/Sub and Cloud Functions Tutorial
resources.why_now: Security automation is critical as environments scale, and this practical tutorial demonstrates how to implement automatic credential rotation using GCP's native Secret Manager rotation notifications and Pub/Sub triggers for Cloud Run functions.

resources.title: App Engine to Cloud Run Migration Now in Preview
resources.why_now: Google is providing a migration path from App Engine standard environment to Cloud Run with the gcloud beta app migrate-to-run command, offering teams a way to modernize legacy App Engine applications to containerized Cloud Run services.

claude_take: GCP is clearly positioning Cloud Run as the future of serverless compute, with GPU support, better cost optimization through flexible CUDs, and migration paths from App Engine. The focus on cost automation and security practices like automatic secret rotation reflects the maturation of cloud-native development patterns where infrastructure management becomes increasingly automated and policy-driven.
